sanskrit: पिताहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः। वेद्यं पवित्रं ओङ्कार ऋक् साम यजुरेव च॥
transliteration: pitāham asya jagato mātā dhātā pitāmahaḥ / vedyam pavitram oṅkāra ṛk sāma yajureva ca
literal_meaning: I am the father, mother, sustainer, and grandfather of this universe; the knowable, the purifier, the sacred syllable Om, and the Ṛg, Sāma, and Yajur Vedas.
contextual_meaning: All relational roles and sacred knowledge are unified in one transcendent Self, dissolving split-off parental introjects into a single internal presence.
psychological_state: Secure existential base; parental imagos reconciled; cognitive schema shifts from fragmented authority figures to cohesive inner Self.
trigger_scenario: Realizing that the critical father-voice and nurturing mother-voice inside your head are both partial aspects of your own adult awareness.
anchor_text: Every role you seek outside—father, mother, protector—already lives complete within you.
